{"key_event":"Khatami reform wave and coercive backlash","reasoning":"Civil society recovered substantially from the war-era low as urban students, women, reformist newspapers, elections, and municipal councils created real mobilizational capacity, though repression after the 1999 protests kept it only moderately powerful. State power stayed very strong: reconstruction and entrenched IRGC, Basij, judiciary, Guardian Council, and bonyad networks offset economic stress and legitimacy losses, allowing the regime to police politics and crush unrest.","society_change":2.0,"society_power":5.0,"state_change":0.2,"state_power":8.2}
